title: Trust-base shrink plan
purpose: Turn the explicit trust base into a prioritized reduction program whose tasks are machine-readable, dependency-tracked, and non-activating.

tasks.title: Replace implicit JavaScript integer assumptions with bounded integer validation
deliverables: integer-boundary validation helpers
negative tests for unsafe integers, overflow-adjacent values, and non-integers
checker audit ledger for all externally supplied integer fields
acceptanceCriteria: Every checker-facing integer field has an explicit finite bound or safe-integer guard.
Mutating a bounded integer outside the permitted range causes reject, not throw.
The one-command verifier includes the integer-boundary audit.

tasks.title: Cross-check stableStringify canonicalization
deliverables: canonical JSON fixture corpus
independent Python stable-stringify implementation
JS/Python canonical-byte agreement tests
acceptanceCriteria: JS and Python canonicalizers agree on the fixture corpus byte-for-byte.
Ambiguous values such as NaN, Infinity, undefined, functions, sparse arrays, and non-plain objects reject or normalize by documented rule.
Digest equality in cross-verifier output binds to canonical bytes, not implementation-local objects.

tasks.title: Add independent SHA256 implementation comparison and checkout manifest replay
deliverables: Python hashlib digest replay over canonical artifacts
JS crypto digest replay over the same byte payloads
cross-digest agreement verdict
detached checkout manifest with file paths and byte digests
acceptanceCriteria: At least the minimal kernel, theorem-binding ledger, trust-base ledger, and no-shared-code policy digest identically under JS and Python.
Digest mismatch causes reject with artifact path and both digests.
The checkout manifest binds the verified file list to byte digests.
The one-command verifier includes the digest-comparison audit.

tasks.title: Replay core proof checks in a second runtime
deliverables: expanded Python verifier for trust-base, release-boundary, and theorem-binding ledgers
no shared code audit extension for additional verifier roots
cross-verifier agreement over accept/reject boundary cases
acceptanceCriteria: Python verifier rejects at least one negative fixture for each replayed checker family.
No independent verifier imports or executes JavaScript checker code.
Cross-verifier verdict lists all replayed families and shared artifact hashes.

tasks.title: Generate Lean or Coq statement skeletons for core semantics
deliverables: formal statement skeleton for NAND direct-wire syntax and evaluation
formal statement skeleton for compatible replacement and slack law
formal statement skeleton for locked NAND threshold and SAT in P implies P equals NP
acceptanceCriteria: Skeleton files are generated from machine-readable source ledgers rather than hand-copied prose.
Each skeleton theorem has a source ledger id and report-binding id.
The skeleton generation audit rejects missing anchors or unbound theorem ids.

tasks.title: Materialize executable NAND direct-wire semantics and small-model tests
deliverables: semantics/nand-direct-wire-spec.json
semantics/nand-direct-wire-reference.mjs
exhaustive small-model tests for bounded NAND DAGs
acceptanceCriteria: The executable spec defines syntax, boundary tuples, output tuples, evaluation, replacement, equivalence, and size.
Small-model tests compare replacement semantics and brute-force equivalence for all bounded instances in the configured range.
Report theorem bindings reference the executable semantics files.

tasks.title: Add SAT encoding truth-table verification for small locked-NAND instances
deliverables: small CNF formula generator
locked NAND construction evaluator for bounded instances
truth-table equivalence tests for satisfiable/unsatisfiable threshold behavior
acceptanceCriteria: For every formula in the configured small universe, brute-force SAT agrees with the locked-NAND threshold predicate.
Satisfiable and unsatisfiable negative mutations are both present.
The audit records the small-universe bound and artifact digest.
